description: Needle-in-a-Haystack Test: 5 synthetic facts with KARL_NEEDLE_xxx tokens
purpose: Tests the system's ability to retrieve and cite specific facts injected at known positions in the corpus. Detects 'Lost in the Middle' failures where information at certain positions in long contexts is missed.

setup_instructions.step_1: Before running this test, inject the synthetic facts below into the ChromaDB corpus at the specified positions.
setup_instructions.step_2: Each needle has a unique KARL_NEEDLE_xxx token that cannot appear naturally in any contract.
setup_instructions.step_3: Run with CAG_ENABLE_FULL_ARTICLE_EXPANSION=False to test raw retrieval without expansion.
setup_instructions.step_4: After testing, remove the injected needles from the corpus.

synthetic_needles.injection_content: KARL_NEEDLE_001: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, employees classified as Senior Night Stockers who have completed ten (10) or more years of continuous service shall receive a one-time annual retention bonus of four hundred fifty dollars ($450.00), payable on the first pay period in June of each contract year.

KARL_NEEDLE_002: In the event of a store-wide technology upgrade (defined as replacement of more than fifty percent (50%) of point-of-sale terminals within a ninety (90) day period), all affected employees shall receive eight (8) hours of paid training at their regular straight-time rate, scheduled within thirty (30) days of the upgrade completion.

KARL_NEEDLE_003: Employees who are required to report to a store location other than their regularly assigned store for inventory purposes shall receive mileage reimbursement at the then-current IRS standard mileage rate for all miles driven in excess of their normal commute distance, provided they submit a completed Mileage Reimbursement Form (MRF-7) within fourteen (14) calendar days of the inventory assignment.

KARL_NEEDLE_004: The Employer shall maintain a minimum of two (2) AED (Automated External Defibrillator) devices per store, inspected quarterly by a certified technician. Employees who are certified AED operators and who use the device in good faith during a workplace medical emergency shall be held harmless from any civil liability arising from such use, to the maximum extent permitted by Colorado law.

KARL_NEEDLE_005: Any employee who is subpoenaed to testify before the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) in a proceeding involving the Employer shall receive their regular straight-time pay for all scheduled hours missed due to such testimony, not to exceed three (3) working days per proceeding. The Employer shall not require the employee to use vacation or personal holiday time for this purpose.

test_cases.question: Is there a retention bonus for Senior Night Stockers with over 10 years of service?
test_cases.success_criteria: Must cite the exact synthetic section and quote the $450 amount

test_cases.question: If my store replaces all the cash registers, do I get training time? How much?
test_cases.success_criteria: Must cite the synthetic section and mention 8 hours of paid training

test_cases.question: I had to drive to another store for inventory. Can I get my gas money back?
test_cases.success_criteria: Must cite the synthetic section and mention the MRF-7 form and 14-day deadline

test_cases.question: Does the store have to have a defibrillator? What if I use it on a customer and something goes wrong?
test_cases.success_criteria: Must cite the synthetic section and explain the hold-harmless protection

test_cases.question: I got called to testify at an NLRB hearing. Do I still get paid for the time I miss at work?
test_cases.success_criteria: Must cite the synthetic section and mention the 3-day limit and no vacation requirement
